seoTitle: Tonganah vs Tranmere — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tonganah and Tranmere. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tonganah and Tranmere.
heading: Tonganah vs Tranmere — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tonganah, Tasmania and Tranmere, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tranmere has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Tonganah (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tonganah or Tranmere safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tonganah has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Tranmere scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tonganah scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Tranmere scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tonganah or Tranmere?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tranmere has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
